title: RoleHasPermissions (spatie pivot, augmented)
description: Spatie's shipped `role_has_permissions` pivot table augmented with denormalised `application_id` + `tenant_id` for query-plan efficiency. Enforced by RoleHasPermissionsObserver — refuses cross-guard + cross-application writes with GuardMismatch / ApplicationMismatch.

x-eloquent.note: This is a Spatie-managed pivot — no dedicated Eloquent model. Spatie exposes it via Role::permissions() belongsToMany + Permission::roles() belongsToMany. Our observer attaches for the enforcement + denormalisation logic.

x-database.description: Spatie ships the pivot with columns (permission_id, role_id). Our migration ADDS (application_id, tenant_id, audit columns).

application_id.description: Denormalised from role.application_id at write time. RoleHasPermissionsObserver enforces role.application_id === permission.application_id when both non-null.

indexes.name: role_has_permissions_pkey
indexes.description: Composite primary key — one permission per role.

indexes.name: role_has_permissions_permission_idx
indexes.description: Reverse lookup: given a permission, what roles hold it?

x-wire.note: Pivot rows are not returned to clients directly. Role.permissions() and Permission.roles() belongsToMany relations return the JOINED permissions/roles respectively.
